root_text.iti18:2.1: “Ekadhammo, bhikkhave, loke uppajjamāno uppajjati bahujanāhitāya bahujanāsukhāya bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ.
root_text.iti18:2.4: Saṅghe kho pana, bhikkhave, bhinne aññamaññaṁ bhaṇḍanāni ceva honti, aññamaññaṁ paribhāsā ca honti, aññamaññaṁ parikkhepā ca honti, aññamaññaṁ pariccajanā ca honti.
root_text.iti18:2.5: Tattha appasannā ceva nappasīdanti, pasannānañca ekaccānaṁ aññathattaṁ hotī”ti.
root_text.iti18:4.1: Ayampi attho vutto bhagavatā, iti me sutanti.

translation_text.iti18:1.1: This was said by the Buddha, the Perfected One: that is what I heard.
translation_text.iti18:2.1: “One thing, mendicants, arises in the world for the detriment and suffering of the people, against the people, for the harm, detriment, and suffering of gods and humans.
translation_text.iti18:2.4: When the Saṅgha is split, they argue, insult, block, and forsake each other.
translation_text.iti18:2.5: This doesn’t inspire confidence in those without it, and it causes some with confidence to change their minds.”
translation_text.iti18:3.2: in a place of loss, in hell.
translation_text.iti18:3.3: Taking a stand against the teaching,
translation_text.iti18:3.4: favoring factions, they ruin their sanctuary.
translation_text.iti18:3.5: After causing schism in a harmonious Saṅgha,
translation_text.iti18:3.6: they burn in hell for an eon.”
translation_text.iti18:4.1: This too is a matter that was spoken by the Blessed One: that is what I heard.

variant_text.iti18:3.4: Yogakkhemā padhaṁsati → yogakkhemā vidhaṁsati (bj, mr); yogakkhemato dhaṁsati (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1)
variant_text.iti18:3.5: bhetvāna → bhitvāna (pts-vp-pli1, mr); bhinditvā (pli-tv-kd17:144 [Paṭhamabhāṇavāra/ Upālipañhā]; an10.39:1 [39. Paṭhamaānandasutta])
